# Strawberry-django Auth
[Django](https://github.com/django/django) registration and authentication with [Strawberry](https://strawberry.rocks/).

## Demo

![Demo Video](https://github.com/nrbnlulu/strawberry-django-auth/blob/main/demo.gif)

## About
#### This Library was inspired by [Django-graphql-auth](https://github.com/pedrobern/django-graphql-auth/).

Abstract all the basic logic of handling user accounts out of your app,
so you don't need to think about it and can **get you up and running faster**.

No lock-in. When you are ready to implement your own code or this package
is not up to your expectations , it's *easy to extend or switch to
your implementation*.


### Docs can be found [here](https://nrbnlulu.github.io/strawberry-django-auth/)

## Features

* [x] Awesome docs!
* [x] Captcha validation (with extra `strawberry-django-auth[captcha]`)
* [x] Async/Sync supported!
* [x] Works with default or custom user model
* [x] Builtin JWT authentication using [PyJWT](https://github.com/jpadilla/pyjwt)
* [x] User registration with email verification
* [x] Retrieve/Update user
* [x] Archive user
* [x] Permanently delete user or make it inactive
* [x] Turn archived user active again on login
* [x] Track user status <small>(archived, verified)</small>
* [x] Password change
* [x] Password reset through email
* [x] Revoke user tokens on account archive/delete/password change/reset
* [x] All mutations return `success` and `errors`
* [x] Default email templates <small>(you will customize though)</small>
* [x] Customizable, no lock-in
* [x] Passwordless registration


### Full schema features

```python


@strawberry.type
class Mutation:
    verify_token = mutations.VerifyToken.field
    update_account = mutations.UpdateAccount.field
    archive_account = mutations.ArchiveAccount.field
    delete_account = mutations.DeleteAccount.field
    password_change = mutations.PasswordChange.field
    swap_emails = mutations.SwapEmails.field

    # these are mutation that does not require authentication.
    captcha = Captcha.field
    token_auth = mutations.ObtainJSONWebToken.field
    register = mutations.Register.field
    verify_account = mutations.VerifyAccount.field
    resend_activation_email = mutations.ResendActivationEmail.field
    send_password_reset_email = mutations.SendPasswordResetEmail.field
    password_reset = mutations.PasswordReset.field
    password_set = mutations.PasswordSet.field
    refresh_token = mutations.RefreshToken.field
    revoke_token = mutations.RevokeToken.field
    verify_secondary_email = mutations.VerifySecondaryEmail.field


schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query, mutation=Mutation)

```

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/nrbnlulu/strawberry-django-auth/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

Creates a new token and possibly a new refresh token based on the
user.

*call this method only for trusted users.*

documentation:
Return `ObtainJSONWebTokenType`. authenticates against django
authentication backends.

*creates a new token and possibly a refresh token.*

methods.name: gqlauth.captcha.models.Captcha.validate
documentation:
Validates input_.

- if tried to validate more than 3 times obj will be deleted in the database
- else increments by one
- if reaches expiery date deletes the obj
- returns bool for success state

methods.name: gqlauth.captcha.models.Captcha.as_bytes

Stores the image on a bytes_array.

The scalar will further convert it to b64 string representation.

classes.name: gqlauth.captcha.create.ImageCaptcha

methods.name: gqlauth.captcha.create.ImageCaptcha.create_captcha_image
documentation:
Create the CAPTCHA image itself.

```
param chars

text to be generated.

param color

color of the text.

param background

color of the background. The color should be
a tuple of 3 numbers, such as (0, 255, 255).
```

methods.name: gqlauth.captcha.create.ImageCaptcha.generate_image
documentation:
Generate the image of the given characters.

```
param chars

text to be generated.
```

Create an image CAPTCHA.

Many of the codes are borrowed from wheezy.captcha, with a modification
for memory and developer friendly.

ImageCaptcha has one built-in font, DroidSansMono, which is licensed under
Apache License 2. You should always use your own fonts:

```
captcha = ImageCaptcha(fonts=['/path/to/A.ttf', '/path/to/B.ttf'])
```

You can put as many fonts as you like. But be aware of your memory, all of
the fonts are loaded into your memory, so keep them a lot, but not too
many.

```
param width

The width of the CAPTCHA image.

param height

The height of the CAPTCHA image.

param fonts

Fonts to be used to generate CAPTCHA images.

param font_sizes

Random choose a font size from this parameters.
```

classes.name: gqlauth.models.UserStatus
documentation: A helper model that handles user account stuff.

classes.name: gqlauth.models.RefreshToken

methods.name: gqlauth.models.RefreshToken.is_expired_
documentation:
Whether the token is expired or not.

it is up to the database query to filter out tokens without
revoked date.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.models.RefreshToken.id
documentation: noqa A003

class_variables.name: gqlauth.models.RefreshToken.objects
documentation: type: ignore

Refresh token is a random set of bytes decoded to a string that is
referring a user.

It can be used to retrieve a new token without the need to login
again.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.LOGIN_FIELDS
documentation:
These fields would be used to authenticate with SD-jwt `authenticate`
function. This function will call each of our `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`,
And will return the user from one of them unless `PermissionDenied` was
raised. You can pass any fields that would be accepted by your backends.

**Note that `password field` is mandatory and cannot be removed.**

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.LOGIN_REQUIRE_CAPTCHA
documentation: Whether login will require captcha verification.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.CI_MODE
documentation:
Whether to enable CI mode, this will disable captcha validation
(although the fields would still be required).

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.REGISTER_MUTATION_FIELDS
documentation:
Fields on register, plus password1 and password2, can be a dict like
UPDATE_MUTATION_fieldS setting.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.REGISTER_REQUIRE_CAPTCHA
documentation: Whether register will require captcha verification.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.CAPTCHA_TEXT_FACTORY
documentation:
A callable with no arguments that returns a string.

This will be used to generate the captcha image.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.CAPTCHA_TEXT_VALIDATOR
documentation:
A callable that will receive the original string vs user input and
returns a boolean.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.FORCE_SHOW_CAPTCHA
documentation:
Whether to show the captcha image after it has been created for
debugging purposes.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.CAPTCHA_SAVE_IMAGE
documentation:
If True, an png representation of the captcha will be saved under
MEDIA_ROOT/captcha/<datetime>/<uuid>.png.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.UPDATE_MUTATION_FIELDS
documentation: Fields on update account mutation.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.ALLOW_DELETE_ACCOUNT
documentation: If True, DeleteAcount mutation will permanently delete the user.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.ALLOW_PASSWORDLESS_REGISTRATION
documentation: Whether to allow registration with no password.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_SECRET_KEY
documentation: Key used to sign the JWT token.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_ALGORITHM
documentation: Algorithm used for signing the token.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_TIME_FORMAT
documentation:
A valid 'strftime' string that will be used to encode the token
payload.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_PAYLOAD_HANDLER
documentation:
A custom function to generate the token datatype, its up to you to
encode the token.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_PAYLOAD_PK
documentation:
Field that will be used to generate the token from a user instance and
retrieve user based on the decoded token. The default value is the username
field of the User model. If you want to change it to id, for example, you
can use the id_field defined in gqlauth.settings_type and change it like
JWT_PAYLOAD_PK=id_field.

*This filed must be unique in the database*

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_TOKEN_FINDER
documentation:
A hook called by `GqlAuthRootField` to find the token. Accepts the
request object (might be channels scope dict or django request object)

**remember to strip the "JWT " prefix if you override this.**

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_EXPIRATION_DELTA
documentation:
Timedelta added to `utcnow()` to set the expiration time.

When this ends you will have to create a new token by logging in or
using the refresh token.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_LONG_RUNNING_REFRESH_TOKEN
documentation:
Whether to enable refresh tokens to be used as an alternative to login
every time the token is expired.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_N_BYTES
documentation: Number of bytes for long running refresh token.

class_variables.name: gqlauth.settings_type.GqlAuthSettings.JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_DELTA
documentation: Refresh token expiration time delta.

functions.name: gqlauth.core.utils.inject_arguments
documentation:
Injects arguments to the decorated resolver.

\:param args:`dict[name, type]` of arguments to be injected.,

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.Captcha
documentation:
Creates a brand-new captcha. Returns a base64 encoded string of the
captcha. And uuid representing the captcha id in the database. When you
will try to log in or register You will need submit that uuid With the
user input.

**The captcha will be invoked when the timeout expires**.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.RegisterMixin
documentation:
Register user with fields defined in the settings. If the email field of
the user model is part of the registration fields (default), check if there
is no user with that email.

If it exists, it does not register the user, even if the email field
is not defined as unique (default of the default django user model).

When creating the user, it also creates a `UserStatus` related to
that user, making it possible to track if the user is archived /
verified.

Send account verification email.

If allowed to not verified users login, return token.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.VerifyAccountMixin
documentation:
Verify user account.

Receive the token that was sent by email. If the token is valid,
make the user verified by making the `user.status.verified` field
true.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.ResendActivationEmailMixin
documentation:
Sends activation email.

It is called resend because theoretically the first activation email
was sent when the user registered.

If there is no user with the requested email, a successful response
is returned.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.SendPasswordResetEmailMixin
documentation:
Send password reset email.

For non verified users, send an activation email instead.

If there is no user with the requested email, a successful response
is returned.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.PasswordResetMixin
documentation:
Change user password without old password.

Receive the token that was sent by email.

If token and new passwords are valid, update user password and in
case of using refresh tokens, revoke all of them.

Also, if user has not been verified yet, verify it.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.PasswordSetMixin
documentation:
Set user password - for password-less registration

Receive the token that was sent by email.

If token and new passwords are valid, set
user password and in case of using refresh
tokens, revoke all of them.

Also, if user has not been verified yet, verify it.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.ObtainJSONWebTokenMixin
documentation:
Obtain JSON web token for given user.

Allow to perform login with different fields, The fields are defined
on settings.

Not verified users can log in by default. This can be changes on
settings.

If user is archived, make it unarchived and return
`unarchiving=True` on OutputBase.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.ArchiveAccountMixin
documentation:
Archive account and revoke refresh tokens.

User must be verified and confirm password.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.DeleteAccountMixin
documentation:
Delete account permanently or make `user.is_active=False`.

The behavior is defined on settings. Anyway user refresh tokens are
revoked.

User must be verified and confirm password.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.PasswordChangeMixin
documentation:
Change account password when user knows the old password.

A new token and refresh token are sent. User must be verified.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.UpdateAccountMixin
documentation:
Update user model fields, defined on settings.

User must be verified.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.VerifyTokenMixin
documentation:
### Checks if a token is not expired and correct.

*Note that this is not for refresh tokens.*

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.RefreshTokenMixin
documentation:
### refreshToken to generate a new login token:

*Use this only if `JWT_LONG_RUNNING_REFRESH_TOKEN` is True*

using the refresh-token you already got during authorization, and
obtain a brand-new token (and possibly a new refresh token if you
revoked the previous). This is an alternative to log in when your
token expired.

classes.name: gqlauth.user.resolvers.RevokeTokenMixin
documentation:
### Suspends a refresh token.

*token must exist to be revoked.*

classes.name: gqlauth.core.directives.IsVerified
documentation: Mark a field as only resolvable by authenticated users.

classes.name: gqlauth.core.middlewares.JwtSchema
documentation: Injects token to context.
